title: Sour Crumbly Festive Ethiopian Pita Bread Flatbread
description: A sour, crumbly, festive Ethiopian pita bread flatbread perfect for serving with injera or just on its own. This dish features a fermented dough made with jang, a traditional Ethiopian spice blend.
instructions: Mix tepache fermented corn water and tepache jang in a large bowl.
Add flour and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat a kadhai or skillet over medium-high heat.
Divide dough into 4-6 pieces and roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Cook flatbread for 1-2 minutes on each side, until it's golden brown and puffed up.
Brush flatbread with a little water and serve warm.
